jobs.description:
Job Description

At CooperVision, a division of CooperCompanies, we’re driven by a unifying purpose to help people to experience life’s beautiful moments. We are connected through our shared values - dedicated, innovative, friendly, partners, and do the right thing. As a leading global manufacturer of contact lenses, we are committed to helping improve the way people see each day. Through our diverse lens portfolio, we tackle the toughest vision challenges – including astigmatism, presbyopia, and childhood myopia. We offer the most complete collection of spherical, toric, and multifocal products available, enabling us to fit 99% of all contact wearers. Learn more at www.coopervision.com.

Job Summary

Data Analyst 1 is an entry-level, developmental role designed to build foundational data analytics and visualization skills in preparation for progression to a Data Analyst 2 role. The position focuses primarily on data collection and visual design activities, with exposure to less complex data analysis and reporting. Visual design may include reworking graphs, applying dashboard templates, and other responsibilities as assigned. This role may also support data recalibrations on a six-month cadence (in Tableau or Excel). The role works under the guidance of senior analysts and follows established metrics and reporting standards to execute tasks. Clear communication, attention to detail, and willingness to learn are essential for success.

Responsibilities

Essential Functions & Accountabilities:

This role focuses on performing basic data analysis, preparing simple reports and visualizations, working on minor tickets (break/fixes, investigations, etc.), and providing support to senior analysts as needed.
Additional responsibilities include collecting, organizing, and validating distribution data to ensure accuracy, completeness, and alignment with established data quality standards and procedures, and visual design within Tableau Desktop, with the goal of enhancing data storytelling and supporting consistent reporting. Visual design includes rebuilding visuals for straightforward reports or developing templates that help senior analysts deliver standardized reporting.

Travel:

Occasional travel may be required (5–10%+ annually). This is an on-site position requiring daily in-office attendance Monday through Friday, and as needed.

Qualifications

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:

Required:

Minimum of 2 years of relevant experience in data collection, data entry, reporting, or analytics support, or
Associate degree with 1 or more years of relevant experience, or
Bachelor’s degree in a related field is preferred, including Data Analytics, Data Science, Computer Science, Mathematics, or Information Technology.
Relevant experience with data collection or data entry required.
Preferred experience in a warehouse setting or manufacturing.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including data organization and basic analysis functions.
0 to 1+ years of experience in SQL (writing simple queries, including joins, filtering, and aggregation).
Basic understanding of analytics (statistical analysis, pattern recognition, data interpretation, problem-solving).
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, SharePoint, etc.)
Willingness to follow standardized departmental, project management, and IT processes.
Ability to work within a team environment.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.

Preferred

0 to 3+ years of experience in Tableau Desktop or PowerBI (or an equivalent data visualization tool).
Experience in Tableau Prep or Alteryx.
Experience writing scripts using Microsoft PowerShell.

Work Environment

On-site, 5 days a week
Relevant experience with data collection or data entry required.
Preferred experience in a warehouse setting or manufacturing.

For U.S. locations that require disclosure of compensation, the starting base pay for this role is between $28,307.00 and $77,743.00 per year and may include cost of living adjustments. The actual base pay includes many factors and is subject to change and modification in the future. This position may also be eligible for other types of compensation and benefits.

jobs.title: Environment Geologist / Scientist
jobs.description:
Woodard & Curran is a national engineering, science and operations firm with a simple vision for clean water, a safe environment, healthy communities and happy people. As an employee-owned company, we strive to cultivate teams that encourage collaboration in an inclusive culture. We seek talented individuals from all backgrounds to help us address a broad range of water and environmental issues. We prioritize our people, offering opportunities for professional growth, ownership and flexibility. This commitment enables us to make a meaningful impact on our clients, communities and the planet.

Woodard & Curran is offering a unique opportunity to join our Mid-Atlantic regional team and provide high-quality environmental services to our clients nationwide. An Environmental Scientist or Geologist is typically assigned specific tasks on project teams and is expected to use their knowledge to resolve the problems and tasks they are assigned under the mentorship of senior staff. Fieldwork, data management , report writing, and interaction with project staff will play an important role in the candidate’s success.

Who are we looking for:

We are looking for a motivated, bright, and talented Environmental Geologist or Scientist to join our company in the Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania area. If you want to jumpstart your career and expand your skills while working alongside talented experts, then this is the opportunity for you! You will be involved with project work from your first day, working side-by-side with experienced engineers, technical specialists, and scientists from across our practice to help clients address their water and environmental challenges. Exercise your creativity and problem-solving skills in a supportive environment where every perspective is valued. We are a mission-driven organization that puts our people first, with a culture that focuses on providing meaningful work, engaging challenges, recognition, and reward.

Location:
Pittsburgh, PA

Travel:
Approximately 50% travel is anticipated

What you will be doing at Woodard & Curran:

Collecting soil, groundwater, and other environmental media samples to support site investigation and remediation objectives.
Installing monitoring wells, performing remediation monitoring, and overseeing contractors.
Assist field investigations and provide construction oversight by following safety policies, thoroughly understanding specifications, and documenting contractor activities and safety procedures.
Assisting in preparing various regulatory submittals, technical memoranda, and/or reports.
Manage, analyze, evaluate, and interpret data for use in reports and other visualizations, including maps, charts, and 3D models.
Candidate should be a self-starter and possess strong technical fieldwork and writing skills to support various science and engineering projects.
Detail-oriented, have excellent verbal communication skills combined with a positive attitude, and can work independently and within teams.

What you will need to succeed:

Bachelor’s degree in geology, environmental science, or related field from an accredited program. M.S. preferred.
Entry level to five years of relevant professional experience.
Experience working around construction equipment and/or in a construction environment is a plus.
Strong computer skills, including Word, Excel, ArcGIS, CAD, or database software, preferred.
Detail-oriented with excellent organizational, writing, communication, and interpersonal skills.
Experience in developing and implementing project work plans, schedules, and budgets is a plus.
40-hour OSHA HAZWOPER training is a plus, but we will train.

$60,000 - $65,000 a year

Anticipated pay range is flexible, depending on factors such as the employee's primary work location, skills, experience, and education.

Mutual Dispute Resolution (MDR)

As part of our commitment to a fair and respectful workplace, Woodard & Curran uses a Mutual Dispute Resolution (MDR)
to help resolve most employment-related concerns efficiently and consistently.

What this means:

MDR applies to both you and the Company
Most workplace-related disputes are resolved through binding arbitration, rather than court proceedings
Certain exceptions are outlined in the full agreement

If you are offered a position with Woodard & Curran, you will be asked to review and sign the MDR as part of your offer packet.

🔗 Review the full agreement here:
Mutual Dispute Resolution Agreement

Employee Support & Benefits

401(k) Pre-tax or Roth Contributions: The company matches 100% of the first 3% of salary contributed

Time away from work:

Observed holidays: Choose up to 8 holidays to observe annually
Vacation: Accrued based on years of experience and calculated on hours worked
Sick time: Paid sick time for non-work-related illness or injury.
Parental leave: Up to 80 hours of Paid Parental Leave for child bonding

Coverage and support for your needs and well-being:

Disability: Paid short- and long-term disability
Health: Medical plan options; plus dental and vision plans.
Life: Basic employee group life insurance and AD&D benefits; additional voluntary life insurance for employees and families is available through Sun Life.
Wellbeing: DarioMind assistance program combined with personalized mental health and wellbeing resources.

jobs.title: Administration Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
THE OPPORTUNITY

Zero West House is a design-build firm creating premium custom homes along the California coast. We are a small, growing company where organized, detail-oriented people make a visible and immediate impact. We work out of a shared creative workspace in Newport Beach where the energy is collaborative and the work is genuinely interesting.
We are looking for a sharp, self-directed Administrative Project Coordinator to sit alongside our founder and become the operational backbone of the company. This is not a siloed admin role. You will be embedded in the business — owning project coordination, financial administration, scheduling, document management, and the systems that keep everything running.
If you are organized by nature, thrive in a fast-moving environment, communicate clearly, and want to grow with a company building something worth being part of — this is the right seat.

THE ROLE

You will work directly with the founder across five areas: project coordination, financial administration, scheduling and calendar management, document and compliance management, and operational systems.
Project Coordination

→ 
Manage and maintain JobTread — our primary project management system — as the single source of truth for all project information
→ 
Create and manage jobs, budgets, schedules, tasks, selections, documents, and files in JobTread
→ 
Track open items, approvals, deadlines, and follow-ups across all active projects
→ 
Coordinate with subcontractors, vendors, and suppliers — follow up on quotes, deliveries, invoices, and open items
→ 
Maintain procurement trackers for long-lead materials — windows, doors, stone, millwork, appliances, and specialty finishes
→ 
Track material orders and delivery timelines — flag anything at risk of affecting the project schedule
→ 
Schedule city inspections and coordinate with the superintendent ahead of each inspection
→ 
Track permit expiration dates and renewal requirements across all active projects
→ 
Support RFIs, change orders, and project closeout documentation
→ 
Keep projects moving by making sure details do not fall through the cracks

Financial Administration

→ 
Process and code expenses in JobTread and sync with QuickBooks Online
→ 
Collect, organize, and upload receipts, invoices, and project-related documents
→ 
Support accounts payable and accounts receivable — track what is owed and what is due
→ 
Assist with transaction processing and basic bookkeeping under monthly bookkeeper oversight
→ 
Maintain clean, accurate financial records and flag discrepancies early
→ 
Coordinate draw requests and documentation for lenders and investors
→ 
Liaise with our external bookkeeper for monthly review and reconciliation

Document + Compliance Management

→ 
Collect and track signed lien waivers from every subcontractor and supplier on every project
→ 
Maintain the sub and vendor rolodex — keep contacts current and add new contacts as the network grows
→ 
Collect and track subcontractor insurance certificates — flag expirations before they lapse
→ 
Send contracts for signature, track execution status, and organize all signed documents
→ 
Maintain organized digital file systems for all project, financial, and legal documents

Scheduling + Calendar Management

→ 
Own the founder's calendar — schedule meetings, calls, site visits, and appointments
→ 
Manage scheduling coordination with subcontractors, vendors, inspectors, and consultants
→ 
Maintain project schedules in JobTread and flag risks before they become problems
→ 
Prepare weekly priorities and agendas to keep the team focused on what matters most

Operational Systems + Admin

→ 
Manage email — triage, draft responses, organize, and follow up on behalf of the founder where appropriate
→ 
Support job postings, applicant screening, interview scheduling, and basic HR coordination
→ 
Draft, send, and track communications with vendors, subcontractors, and partners
→ 
Identify gaps in systems and suggest better ways to keep operations running smoothly
→ 
Help build repeatable workflows, templates, and checklists as the company scales

WHO THIS IS FOR

This role is for someone who has already proven they can hold a lot of moving parts without dropping any of them.
→ 
An experienced administrative professional who has supported a founder or executive in a small company
→ 
Someone with a background in construction, real estate, design, or a related industry — or sharp enough to learn it fast
→ 
A detail-driven operator who takes full ownership and does not need to be managed
→ 
Someone who communicates clearly, follows through consistently, and treats every vendor and partner with professionalism
→ 
A person who finds genuine satisfaction in bringing order to complexity and keeping a high-performing team organized

WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

→ 
3+ years of experience in an administrative, project coordination, operations, or executive assistant role
→ 
Experience in construction, real estate, design-build, or a related industry strongly preferred
→ 
Strong financial administration skills — comfortable with QuickBooks Online, expense tracking, AP/AR, and basic bookkeeping
→ 
Familiarity with construction compliance — lien waivers, insurance certificates, permits — preferred
→ 
High proficiency in Google Workspace — Gmail, Google Drive, Google Calendar, Sheets
→ 
Experience with project management software — JobTread experience ideal but not required. We pay for and support your JobTread certification during onboarding.
→ 
Exceptional written communication — professional, clear, and concise
→ 
Highly organized with strong attention to detail and consistent follow-through
→ 
Able to work independently, manage competing priorities, and stay composed under pressure
→ 
Comfortable coordinating with vendors and subcontractors by phone and email
→ 
Must be based in or able to commute to Newport Beach, CA

COMPENSATION + BENEFITS

→ 
Base salary: $60,000–$75,000 depending on experience
→ 
JobTread certification paid by ZWH — a genuine professional development investment
→ 
10 days PTO plus standard paid holidays
→ 
Direct access to the founder and real input into how the company operates and scales
→ 
Health insurance stipend under consideration as the company grows

WHY ZERO WEST HOUSE

Zero West House is not a large construction company with layers of management and bureaucracy. We are a design-driven firm building exceptional homes on the California coast — and we are in the early stages of something worth being part of.
The person who takes this role will have direct access to the founder, real ownership over their area of the business, and the opportunity to grow alongside a company that values quality, clarity, and craft. If you make the people around you more effective and find meaning in building something from the ground up — we want to meet you.

About Zero West House

Zero West House is a creatively driven design-build firm specializing in premium Southern California style homes built near the coast. We form it, design it, and build it — one team, one vision, from first sketch to completed build. Where the soul lives.

new_jobs.title: Data Analyst
new_jobs.description:
Harper is an AI-native commercial insurance company in San Francisco.
We're not bolting AI onto insurance — we're rebuilding the entire business as software, on a simple bet: turning expert human judgment into compute is one of the largest transitions left to make, and a trillion-dollar industry still run 90% by hand is the place to prove it. We've grown ~100x in the last year and we move at that speed — on-site, in person, long days, very high standards. Almost no one joins Harper for insurance; they join to build the company that replaces how it works.

The role

Harper's GTM Engineer builds the data infrastructure. You're the one who tells us what it's saying. Every morning you'll wake up asking the same question: what does the data want us to do today? You sit closest to our Growth Marketing Lead, synthesizing signal from across every channel and turning it into clear, confident recommendations they can act on. You're not a report generator — you're an analytical partner who helps drive decisions, translating the noise into the two or three things that actually matter and helping the team move on them.

You're a growth-oriented data analyst who's as comfortable drawing a conclusion as you are running the query that supports it. You've worked closely with marketing or growth teams before — not as a data service desk, but as a real partner in the work. You know what good channel performance looks like, you understand the metrics that matter in paid acquisition, and you can translate a cohort analysis into a budget recommendation without anyone having to ask. You report to the Head of Marketing, work day-to-day alongside the Growth Marketing Lead, and collaborate closely with the GTM Engineer who owns the underlying data systems. Winning in 6–12 months looks like the growth team making better decisions faster because you're in the room.

What You'll Do

Synthesize channel performance. Pull together data across paid, organic, and partner channels — and identify what's working, what's not, and what to do about it.
Partner with the Growth Marketing Lead. Serve as their analytical right hand; translate data outputs into clear recommendations they can act on in real time.
Own growth KPI reporting. Define, maintain, and communicate core marketing and funnel metrics so the team is always looking at the right numbers.
Build and maintain LTV/CAC analysis. Give leadership a clear, current picture of unit economics by channel, cohort, and customer segment.
Run experiment analysis. Evaluate A/B tests and campaign experiments — and turn results into decisions, not slide decks.
Surface what the data is asking for. Proactively flag trends, anomalies, and opportunities before anyone has to ask you to look.

Who You Are

You've worked as an analytical partner to a marketing or growth team — not just supported them from a distance.
You can draw a clear conclusion from messy data and defend it in a room with people who will push back.
You think in recommendations, not just findings — you're not done until you've said "and therefore we should…"
You're fast: you can turn a question into an answer in hours, not days.
You understand paid acquisition channels well enough to have opinions about where the budget should go.
You thrive in a high-tempo environment where the questions change faster than the data does.
You're based in San Francisco or willing to relocate.

The reality — read this before you apply

This is a decision-driving seat, measured by the decisions your analysis changes — not dashboards shipped or tickets closed. It's on-site in San Francisco, in person, long days, high standards. The questions move fast and the data is messy, which means you'll be expected to draw a conclusion and stand behind it, often before the picture is fully clean. You're the analytical engine for the growth team, so when a number looks wrong or a channel turns, finding out why is yours — frequently the same day.

This is explicitly not a data service desk, and it isn't a place to produce reports no one acts on. Almost no one takes it for insurance — they take it because being the analytical right hand to a growth team at a company scaling ~100x, with a direct line from your recommendation to where the budget goes, is a rare seat. If being measured on decisions is the appeal, apply. If it's a deterrent, it won't get easier here.

Compensation & Logistics

Salary: $140,000–$180,000, plus performance bonus and equity.
Location: On-site, San Francisco. Based here or willing to relocate.
Schedule: Monday–Friday, long days, in the building with the team.

Benefits

Uber commuter benefits
Meals provided — breakfast, lunch, and dinner
Snacks, drinks, and coffee stocked daily
Free gym membership
Health, dental, and vision insurance

Requirements

4+ years of analytics experience with direct exposure to marketing, growth, or revenue teams
Strong SQL; Python proficiency preferred
Hands-on experience with funnel analysis, cohort analysis, attribution, and LTV/CAC modeling
Experience analyzing paid acquisition channel performance (Google, Meta, or comparable)
Proven ability to communicate analytical findings clearly to non-technical stakeholders and drive decisions
Experience partnering with a marketing or growth counterpart to act on data in real time — not just produce reports

Nice to have

Familiarity with PostHog, Mixpanel, Amplitude, or comparable product analytics platforms
Experience working alongside a data engineer or GTM engineer and understanding how data pipelines work
Background in insurance, fintech, or another industry with complex funnel and attribution dynamics
Exposure to AI-assisted analytics workflows and modern BI tooling
Experience in a high-growth startup environment where speed and adaptability were required

Process

People screen — fit and alignment
Lead screen — skills and judgment
Super day — how you operate in real time

To apply

Send your resume and an example of analysis you ran — what you recommended, and what the team did with it.

Compensation Range: $140K - $180K

Build the future of the Data Cloud. Join the Snowflake team.

Our Solution Engineering organization is seeking an Analytics Architect who can provide hands-on expertise and support while working with technical decision makers to design and architect analytical solutions built on the Snowflake Data Cloud.

This is a strategic role that works closely with cross-functional teams, including product, engineering, and the broader field organization to ensure successful execution and customer adoption of Snowflake’s solutions.

IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL GET TO:

Convey the strategic advantages and technical application of Snowflake's Analytics capabilities, address client inquiries, and conduct follow-up engagements to facilitate sales progression.
Articulate Snowflake's Analytics functionalities and provide a comparative analysis with competitor offerings for prospective clients.

Provide comprehensive resolutions to client objections and concerns.
Develop and deliver bespoke demonstrations that highlight Snowflake's value proposition, tailored to address specific client requirements pertaining to analytic functionalities.
Demonstrate the ability to elucidate Snowflake's security, privacy, and governance considerations with respect to Analytics.
Be the technical expert in the room that positions Snowflake’s analytical features and value to technical stakeholders at Snowflake’s customers across the Americas.
Function as the principal technical authority, articulating Snowflake's analytical functionalities and value proposition to technical stakeholders within customer organizations across the Americas.
Collaborate with Snowflake account teams and customer advocates to define and execute Proof of Concepts, ensuring successful outcomes and demonstrable technical achievements that validate Snowflake's capabilities, including comprehensive executive summaries and business value assessments.
Develop and disseminate content to facilitate team and organizational growth, such as blog articles, conference presentations, and technical materials including notebooks and demonstrations.
Act as the Voice of Customer by triaging Product gaps and communicating their priority to Product Management.
Develop sales programs with Product Marketing around GTM for Analytics features.

ON DAY ONE, WE WILL EXPECT YOU TO HAVE:

5+ years Data architecture experience
Curiosity and drive to help companies leverage the Snowflake Data Cloud
Strong presentation skills to both technical and executive audiences, whether whiteboarding sessions or formal readouts and demos.
Bachelor’s Degree required, Masters Degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics or related fields, or equivalent experience preferred.
Knowledge of and experience with large-scale database technology (e.g. Snowflake, Netezza, Exadata, Teradata, Greenplum, etc.)

BONUS POINTS FOR EXPERIENCE WITH THE FOLLOWING:

Experience with other analytical tools, ie Dremio, Athena
Experience with Iceberg
Experience with Geospatial and/or Time series analytics
1+ years of practical Snowflake experience.

new_jobs.title: Program Coordinator
new_jobs.description:
Job Announcement: June 22nd 2026
Application Due Date: July 12th, 2026
Anticipated Start Date: August 1st, 2026

HOW TO APPLY:

Submit a cover letter with two professional references and a resume to:
E-mail (preferred method): Michelle@issaquahfoodbank.org
Mail:
Michelle Whalen
Issaquah Food & Clothing Bank 179 1st Avenue SE Issaquah, WA 98027
Please do not call, thank you!

JOB DESCRIPTION:

· Full-time (40 hrs/week), hourly
· Monday-Friday, occasional weekend or evening shifts
· Onsite
· Reports to: Program Supervisor

COMPENSATION & BENEFITS:

· $ 30-32/hr DOE
· Paid sick and vacation time
· Health, vision, and dental insurance plans (85% covered by IFCB)
· Simple IRA plan with matching
· Holiday time off

ABOUT US:

In 2021, we celebrated our 50th anniversary, focusing on the community compassion which makes us the vital resource we’ve become for thousands of families. The staff, volunteers, donors, and community partners of the Issaquah Food & Clothing Bank help our neighbors stay healthy, employed, and housed by providing free food, hygiene and household items, case management and resource referral services. We believe that food is a right, not a privilege, and dignity is at the heart of everything we do. Our goal is to provide a gracious space where people feel welcome, safe, and cared for. If you are passionate
about this mission and those who find themselves in need of these resources, please consider joining our team of compassionate and driven professionals.

POSITION SUMMARY:

As Program Coordinator, you are an essential part of a highly collaborative team that executes IFCB Special Programs, including personalized home deliveries, youth nutrition support, holiday gifts, and a partnership with KidVantage. Coordination includes logistics, communication, and outreach with clients and partners.
This position is involved in all Special Programs but will largely focus on the operational needs of programs, and management of our home delivery program. Driving staff vehicles (vans and box truck) is required for this position. Applicants must have a clear driving record, pass a background check, and be at least 25 years old to meet the insurance coverage requirements for staff vehicles.
Candidates must be self-motivated, detail oriented, organized, and effective at working with diverse volunteers and small teams. The ideal candidate for this role has excellent communication skills, is a creative problem solver, and is committed to providing a high level of customer service.
For a program coordinator, success looks like executing projects timely, working collaboratively with coworkers, clients, and stakeholders, helping the department reach goals, demonstrating the ability to support clients accessing services, and organizing and training volunteers to execute programs.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

1. Coordinate daily IFCB operations, particularly in regard to Special Programs, including:
· Lead teams of volunteers in the completion of various tasks.
· Drive box truck or large van as needed for food/product pickups.
· Support regular operations of the food bank, including distribution of food in our market.
· Co-lead one or two evening shopping shifts (11:30 am – 7:30 pm) a month.
· Assist with setting up and prepping workspaces for home delivery and youth programs.
· Working closely with the Operations team for daily details and process improvement.

2. Coordinate program logistics and administrative tasks, including:
· Serve as primary staff lead during special program events and activities, including appropriate set up, program administration, and needed reset after program completion.
· Work with Program Supervisor or other staff to place orders, maintain knowledge of current food supplies, and integrate existing inventory for Special Programs.
· In conjunction with volunteer staff team, ensure appropriate volunteer engagement, staffing, and leadership for program volunteers.
· Regularly update client home delivery orders.
· Oversee, track, and report out on program registration.
· Data entry and administrative program support.
· Work to ensure efficient procedures for home delivery, order packing and delivery.
· Develop program fliers, client registration materials, and post-event communications.
· Assist with tracking and evaluating program performance and outcomes.

3. Provide excellent communication and customer service across IFCB programs and services.
· Manage client communications, including responding promptly to client inquiries and assisting clients with IFCB programs and service registration.
· Assist with answering front door and addressing community members’ emergency needs.
· Regularly communicate with agency partners on program services and identify new partnership opportunities.
· Collaborate on improving and refining program and operation procedures.

4. Develop and maintain positive, collaborative working relationships with team members, volunteers, donors, and other partner agencies.

5. Perform other duties as assigned by the manager and attend training as required.

QUALIFICATIONS:

· Required:
o Associate or bachelor’s degree and two (2) years of program coordination or customer service experience preferred. In lieu of a degree, relevant experience considered.
o Applicants must have a valid driver’s license, clean driving record, pass a driving and criminal background check, and be at least 25 years old to meet the insurance coverage requirements for staff vehicles.
o Must be able to lift up to 50 pounds on a regular basis. This is a physically rigorous position in a busy office and warehouse environment, and occasionally in adverse weather conditions.

· Strongly desired:
o Bilingual in Spanish, Mandarin, Russian or Ukrainian.
o Experience with operating a pallet jack, or willingness to learn.
o Experience driving a box truck and large van, or willingness to learn.
o Experience working in a food pantry or similar operation.

Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities

· Strong focus on details and accuracy in all aspects of work.
· Commitment to quality customer service.
· Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal skills.
· Ability to work effectively with a diverse community of staff, volunteers, and clients.
· Exercise good judgement and discretion, maintain confidentiality of clients, colleagues, and agency.
· Ability to plan, initiate, and manage multiple tasks despite frequent interruptions with accuracy, flexibility, and professionalism.
· Work as part of a team, as well as work independently with minimal supervision.
· Proficiency with Microsoft Office Word, Excel, and Outlook.

new_jobs.title: Junior Geospatial Analyst
new_jobs.description:
DecisionPoint Corporation is seeking a Junior Geospatial Analyst
to support the United States Strategic Command (USSTRATCOM) J2 Intelligence Directorate. This role supports mission-essential geospatial intelligence operations focused on analyzing and visualizing potential adversaries’ strategic weapons capabilities.

This position is onsite at Offutt AFB.

Duties & Responsibilities

Support the Government in developing a comprehensive understanding of potential adversary's strategic weapons via Spatial Analysis and Support.
Provide cartographic and spatial analytic support to USSTRATCOM J2 and employ Geographic Information Systems (GIS) expertise to identify, analyze, and depict intelligence issues spatially.
Assist with analyzing area limitation and mobility models pertaining to potential adversary's military weapon systems and demonstrate knowledge of the influential factors pertaining to this problem set.
Must also have experience with the installation, configuration, operation, and limitations of the ArcGIS Enterprise.
Use the ArcGIS Enterprise to produce web maps and web mapping applications and shall possess the experience required to employ adapters in the ArcGIS GeoEvent Processor in order to process DoD informational feeds for incorporation into a situational awareness tracker and geospatial analysis dashboard.
Advise and assist the Government on best practices for information sharing to support day-to-day operations, crisis, and exercises with a focus on improving the tracker and dashboard through application improvement and day-to-day maintenance.
Assist with designing the digital production environment and advisement of business processes and information flow improvement, including automating the collection, organization, dissemination, and archival of J2 products for all J2 web presences. Additionally, support is required for J23 map/chart requirements management.
Support to J23 will include employing Geodatabases and Python scripts to manage requirements and production of standard aeronautical, topographic, and hydrographic products and data by external Combat Support Agencies.
Use ArcGIS Enterprise software to provide support to develop databases, create custom maps, charts, and graphics, and leverage Combat Support Agencies and other DoD visualization system capabilities.

Qualifications

Active Top Secret (TS) security clearance with eligibility for Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI).
Bachelor’s degree in Geospatial Sciences, Geography, Remote Sensing, Geospatial Intelligence, or a related field with a concentration in Geographic Information Systems (GIS) or Geographic Information Science; or
Four (4) years of military experience in Remote Sensing, Geospatial Intelligence, Geographic Information Systems (GIS), or Geographic Information Science in lieu of a degree.

A minimum of three (3) years of demonstrated experience in Geographic Information Systems (GIS), with an emphasis on data editing, spatial analysis, and the use of ArcGIS Pro.
A minimum of three (3) years of relevant experience with GIS data management practices and a foundational understanding of ArcPy scripting.
Demonstrated ability to quickly learn, adapt, and grow into more complex technical roles.

Skills and Competencies

Demonstrated proficiency in the following technologies and platforms:
ArcGIS Enterprise Portal
ArcGIS Pro
GeoEvent Server
ArcPy
Python scripting

Experience with Department of Defense (DoD) IT security standards, including but not limited to DISA STIGs and FedRAMP.
Demonstrated experience working with spatial databases, such as PostgreSQL/PostGIS and Microsoft SQL Server.
Experience with dynamic data connections, including REST APIs and similar technologies, as well as system integration.

Pay Transparency Policy: In accordance with Presidential Executive Order 13665, DecisionPoint Corporation will not discharge or in any other manner discriminate against employees or applicants because they have inquired about, discussed, or disclosed their own pay or the pay of another employee or applicant. Authorization to Share Resume and Personal Information: By expressing your interest and submitting your resume for this position, you authorize DecisionPoint Corporation to share your resume, as well as personal information included on the resume, with its subsidiaries, affiliates and teaming partners for the purpose of considering you for this position and other available positions requiring comparable skills, education and experience. Position Title

Job Description

If this opportunity aligns with your strengths, background, and commitment to fostering inclusivity, we encourage you to apply! At the Oregon Health Authority (OHA), we value diversity and encourage applicants from all backgrounds and experiences to help us build a stronger, more equitable future for all.

Opportunity Awaits, Apply Today! Health Cost Data Analyst (RA3)

As a Health Cost Data Analyst, you will support the development, maintenance, and administration of the Sustainable Health Care Cost Growth Target Program. You will collect, analyze, and report data from health insurers, hospitals, and health care providers, including health care claims, encounter, and enrollment data, to produce summary statistics, performance measures, and other indicators used to assess cost growth targets and identify areas for improvement.

In this role, you will oversee data quality and validation, report generation, data request fulfillment, and ad hoc analyses for internal and external partners. You will also plan and manage key initiatives related to data analysis, validation, and internal process improvement.

Additionally, you will collaborate closely with analysts in the Office of Health Analytics, program staff in the Office of Health Policy, and partners across OHA divisions such as Public Health and Health Systems, as well as external partners including the Department of Human Services (DHS) and the Department of Consumer and Business Services (DCBS).

For a full review of the position description, please
click here.

What We Are Looking For

Minimum Qualifications:
These qualifications must be reflected in your application for consideration.

A Bachelor's Degree in any discipline that included six-quarter units in statistics or quantitative analysis methods and procedures, and one year experience using computerized applications to independently gather, compile, and analyze data and prepare narrative or statistical reports;

OR

Four years of research experience using computerized applications to independently gather, compile, and analyze data and prepare narrative or statistical reports.

Desired Attributes:
The following are desired attributes that we have identified as key to success for this role at OHA. These are the candidate attributes we are searching for. If you possess the following candidate attributes, please let us know in your application.

Graduate-level coursework in health sciences, economics, statistics, informatics, public policy, public health, or computer science preferred.
At least two years of experience analyzing health care financial, claims, or enrollment data.
Knowledge of health care claims, encounter, enrollment data, health care quality, cost, and utilization measures.
Proficiency with statistical software (e.g., R or SAS) and programming languages (e.g., SQL).
Demonstrated ability to write scripts or macros to automate data extraction and compilation from Excel or flat files.
Demonstrated ability to perform statistical and quantitative analyses using health services research and evaluation methods.
Demonstrated ability to present data using written summaries, charts, graphs, and data visualizations (e.g., Tableau or similar tools).
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple competing priorities in a complex, deadline-driven environment, with knowledge of health policy (including Medicaid, Medicare, and health reform) and experience supporting culturally responsive and inclusive work environments.

Application Guidance

Submission Requirements

Your candidate profile and application are your opportunity to showcase your skills and interest in this role. Submissions will be reviewed for clarity, consistency, and communication skills, and for alignment with the guidance provided in the below:

Resume – Attach your current resume that clearly outlines your learned and professional (including volunteer) experience, education, and work experience relevant to this position.
Cover Letter – A cover letter is required and will be reviewed and considered during the review and selection process. Please include specific examples describing how your experience aligns with the desired qualifications for this position. Applications without cover letters will not be considered.
Supplemental Questions – Answer all supplemental questions completely and accurately.
Final Check! – Before submitting, confirm that all required materials are included, and your Workday Job History is current. The information in your application will be used to complete an Equal Pay Analysis to determine salary placement upon hire. Visit the Equal Pay Analysis Webpage for more information.

How To Apply

External Candidates: Visit the State of Oregon job opportunities webpage or click the “Apply” tab above to submit your application, following all submission requirements.
Internal Candidates: Current State of Oregon employees must apply through their employee Workday login, following all submission requirements.

After You Apply

Complete any pending tasks in Workday under “My Applications” prior to the application deadline.
Check your email (including junk folder) and Workday inbox regularly for updates on your application.

Reminders

Eligible veterans and Oregon National Guard servicemembers (current and former) who meet the qualifications of the position will be awarded candidate preference. To protect your information and ensure correct processing, please follow the instructions for submitting your Veterans’ Preference documentation.
Please download and save a copy of this job announcement and linked position description, as it is not available after the application deadline.

About The Team & Benefits Of Joining

We offer a workplace that balances productivity with enjoyment; promotes an atmosphere of mutual respect, dedication, and enthusiasm. You will collaborate with a team of smart, experienced and passionate people.

Benefits

If you are seeking work that is meaningful and changes lives, the Oregon Health Authority may be the place for you. We also offer a competitive benefits package including:

Excellent, low-cost medical, vision, and dental coverage for you and your family.
Optional benefits like life insurance, disability coverage, deferred compensation, and FSA options for health and childcare.
Generous paid time off: 11 holidays, 3 personal days, monthly sick leave, and vacation accrual starting at 8 hours/month.

new_jobs.title: Senior Data Analyst, RegTech
new_jobs.description:
About Us

DriveWealth is on a mission to make investing easier. We believe that everyone should have the ability to control their financial future, and that access to financial markets should not be limited by geography, wealth, or legacy systems. We are a global B2B financial technology organization dedicated to democratizing access to financial independence around the world. Our mission is realized through an API-based platform, empowering our partners to offer seamless investing and trading experiences to clients worldwide, all from their mobile devices. Our technology provides partners with a modern, extensible toolkit, enabling traditional investment workflows and innovative techniques like fractional share ownership. DriveWealth has evolved into a global platform offering trading of US equities, mutual funds, ETFs, fixed income, and options.

There’s never been a better time to build a category-defining business and there has rarely been a team better positioned for this opportunity. Our culture blends the pace and agility of a fintech start-up with the impact, stability, and discipline of Wall Street. We encourage creativity and experimentation while ensuring institutional-grade execution and regulatory compliance in everything we do. Join us and help build the future of global investing!

About The Role:

The RegTech team at DriveWealth develops data products essential for our compliance department to meet all regulatory obligations. We are seeking a Senior Data Analyst to focus on the creation, maintenance, and monitoring of highly curated data marts, feeds, and dashboards for both internal compliance and external regulatory reporting.

The ideal candidate must possess a deep understanding of the trade lifecycle and have a proven track record of collaboration with data engineers, product managers, and regulatory stakeholders. The role entails ownership of data quality, including defining quality standards, developing and implementing comprehensive testing strategies, and establishing robust data governance frameworks to ensure accuracy, integrity, and compliance across all regulatory data assets.

What You’ll Do:

Support Ad-Hoc Inquiries and Regulatory Exams: Respond quickly and accurately to ad-hoc data requests and regulatory examination inquiries from Compliance and Legal teams, providing data and analysis as needed.
Own the Regulatory and Supervisory Data Layer: Architect, build, and maintain data marts and Sigma reports used for critical regulatory functions (e.g., CAT, EBS, trade monitoring, AML) and internal compliance supervisory monitoring, ensuring consistent, accurate outputs delivered quickly through efficient code with robust testing and verification.
Drive Optimization and Efficiency: Continuously evaluate and optimize data models, SQL queries, and Sigma reports for performance, cost-efficiency, and scalability, contributing to a best-in-class RegTech data infrastructure
Proactively Detect and Report Upstream Data Anomalies: Establish monitoring and alerting to proactively detect, analyze, and escalate upstream data anomalies and pipeline failures, providing daily exception reports to upstream data producers.
Own the Business Logic Layer: Define, implement, and rigorously document the core business logic and transformations within the data marts to ensure accurate calculation and aggregation for all regulatory and supervisory metrics
Monitor Inbound Regulatory Files and Feeds: Establish and maintain robust monitoring for all critical inbound regulatory data files and real-time feeds, ensuring timely arrival, data quality, and correct ingestion into the data ecosystem.
Translate Regulation to Data: Work directly with business partners and Compliance to translate new regulatory rules, changes, and reporting obligations into precise analytics engineering requirements and data flows.
Lead Comprehensive Data Testing: Team with QA to architect and implement a rigorous, automated testing framework, including building and maintaining dbt data tests to validate transformation logic and data integrity, developing unit tests for all critical business logic, and establishing automated regression testing to ensure zero defect releases for all regulatory data products.

What You’ll Need

Deep domain knowledge in FinTech/Capital Markets, including a strong understanding of the trade/order lifecycle (Orders, Executions, Allocations) and US regulatory reporting requirements.
Proven deep experience implementing, maintaining, and monitoring data for core regulatory processes (e.g., CAT, EBS, trade monitoring, AML).
Experience working within a larger data team, balancing ideating on solutions, creating output, writing verification tests, and reviewing others' work.
Fluent in SQL.
Experience with building business intelligence reports (Sigma Computing is preferred)

Bonus points for

Python (Jupyter Notebooks)
DBT
NoSQL and semi-structured data
Familiarity with Databricks, AWS, Airflow, GitHub, and Agile development
Experience documenting and maintaining procedures for regulatory purposes.

Compensation package offerings are based on candidate experience and technical qualifications, as it relates to the role. These are identified and determined throughout your interviewing experience.Please note: this role is expected to come into our office on a cadence set by the Hiring Manager/Team.

New York, NY (Hybrid) Pay Range: $150,000 USD - $180,000 USD

Working at DriveWealth

We do our best work when we’re in the same room. To maintain the speed our partners expect, our New York, Chicago, and Lithuania teams work in-office 4 days a week. We’ve found that being physically side-by-side is the only way to solve complex problems in real-time and stay truly accountable to the products we ship. When you’re here, you’re working directly with the people making the decisions. To support that work, we provide competitive compensation, equity, and a 401(k) match. We also offer full insurance coverage, a wellness reimbursement, a company-provided phone, and a personal development allowance. Finally, we value the time you spend away from the office with generous PTO, observed holidays, and extended leave.

Work Authorization

Applicants must possess the legal right to work in the country where the position is located at the time of application. DriveWealth requires all employees to provide original documentation verifying their work authorization on or before their first day of employment.

For US-based roles: Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis without the need for current or future visa sponsorship. DriveWealth does not provide visa sponsorship or support for employment authorization, including transfers, at this time. Offers of employment are strictly contingent upon an individual’s ability to secure and maintain the legal right to work at the Company.

How We Think About AI

We leverage AI to work smarter and move faster. We seek AI-curious talent who are proactive about using emerging tools to increase signal quality, reduce friction, and improve outcomes to deliver products faster, provide better service to our partners, and to streamline processes. Your ability to leverage our internal tools and technology to drive results is as important to us as your core domain expertise.

Compensation

Pay is generally based on the level, complexity, responsibility, location, and job duties/requirements of the specific position. We then source candidates with the requisite skills, expertise, education, training, and experience. If you are selected for an interview, please feel welcome to speak to a recruiter about our compensation philosophy and other available benefits. This role is eligible for base, bonus, equity, 401(k) match, and heavily subsidized benefits and perks.

Equal Employment Opportunity

To build technology and products that are used and loved by people and solve real-world problems, we need to build a team with many different perspectives and experiences. We are an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply.

Agency Disclaimer

DriveWealth does not accept agency resumes. Do not forward resumes to our jobs alias, employees, or any other organization location. DriveWealth is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes.

Data Analyst

About Hippo:

Hippo was built on a promise: make homeownership effortless. Nearly a decade later, that mission still drives us. We use technology and data to help our customers stay ahead of problems and protect what matters most.

Today, that same tech-native approach powers our work beyond homeowners. Hippo operates as a diversified carrier platform, partnering with MGAs to deliver tailored program solutions that help them grow and deliver better customer experiences. Behind that work is a team that values ownership, curiosity, collaboration, and continuous improvement.

If you're energized by building what's next, we'd love to meet you.

About This Role:

The Data Analyst will play a key role in supporting Hippo’s Business Enablement & Insights function by driving analytics across Portfolio Management and Partner Programs, helping business leaders understand portfolio performance, profitability, risk exposure, partner performance, and operational outcomes to inform strategic decision-making and drive business growth.

This role goes beyond traditional reporting—focusing on translating complex data into actionable insights that drive profitable growth, operational efficiency, and risk-aware decision-making. The analyst will partner closely with leaders across business domains to monitor performance, diagnose drivers, and identify opportunities to optimize outcomes across the business. 

As part of a centralized analytics team, this individual will help ensure consistent metrics, strong data foundations, and high-impact insights that directly support Hippo’s strategic and financial goals. 

About You:

You have a bias for action and enjoy driving initiatives from insight to impact — innovating not just for the sake of it, but to move the needle on outcomes that matter. You bring strong analytical skills and experience in business analytics across growth (GTM) and/or insurance domains such as retention, underwriting, and claims. You act like an owner: going beyond reporting to identify trends, diagnose root causes, and recommend clear, actionable next steps. You are detail-oriented and proactive, continuously looking for opportunities to improve performance. You thrive in cross-functional environments, embrace diverse perspectives to sharpen your thinking, and communicate insights in ways that resonate with stakeholders across the business — because you know that the best results come from being in it together.

What You’ll Do:

Build and maintain dashboards to track performance across growth and insurance KPIs (e.g., risk aggregation, retention, loss ratio, claims efficiency, underwriting performance).
Analyze trends and performance drivers across portfolio performance, insurance programs, underwriting, claims, risk exposure, and financial results, surfacing insights that impact growth, profitability, and strategic decision-making
Identify root causes of performance fluctuations and proactively highlight areas requiring action.
Partner with stakeholders across GTM and insurance functions to inform data-driven strategies and operational decisions.
Support retention and lifecycle analytics, including churn drivers, LTV, segmentation, and customer experience insights.
Contribute to claims and underwriting analytics, identifying opportunities to improve efficiency, risk selection, and loss outcomes.
Collaborate with Technology and Data Engineering teams to define data requirements and improve data quality, tracking, and accessibility.
Develop executive-ready reports and presentations with clear insights and recommended actions.
Support forecasting, scenario modeling, and performance tracking against AOP and strategic targets.

Must Haves:

5-8 years of experience in business analytics, strategy, or operations (experience in insurance or insurtech is a plus).
Strong analytical and technical skills (SQL, Excel, Tableau).
Experience working with performance metrics across growth and/or insurance domains (e.g., marketing funnels, retention, claims, underwriting).
Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for senior stakeholders.
Strong communication skills and ability to influence cross-functional partners.
A proactive problem solver who takes ownership and drives outcomes.
Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, dynamic environment with evolving priorities.

Benefits and Perks:

Hippo treats its team members with the same level of dedication and care as we do our customers, which is why we’re fortunate to provide all of our Hippos with:

Hippo Habitat - Snacks and drinks available and catered lunches for onsite employees

new_jobs.description:
At Materion, everyone is included, respected and offered opportunity to grow. Join us!

The Sustainability Data Analyst will be responsible for collecting, compiling, calculating, analyzing, and reporting data related to Materion’s global sustainability performance. Areas of focus include greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions, energy consumption (electricity, natural gas, fuels), renewable energy, water usage, waste and recycling, pollution, customer/product-footprints, and other key environmental metrics across our operations. This position will also be responsible for continuously improving sustainability data management systems to enhance accuracy, efficiency, transparency, and auditability.

You Will Have An Opportunity To

Facilitate the development of the annual, corporate-wide sustainability inventory in alignment with applicable reporting/compliance standards. Work includes distributing internal data surveys, collecting and compiling site-level data, identifying and resolving data errors and outliers, aggregating data, applying unit conversions and emissions factors, creating summary tables, and developing data visualizations.
Manage corporate-wide Energy Metrics data program, including extracting energy data from external/internal databases, manually gathering missing data, compiling energy data and metrics, and disseminating information to operational teams. Work includes tracking savings from energy reduction initiatives by gathering project data from operations teams and assisting with energy savings calculations.
Support development of product-level carbon footprint data for key customers consistent with applicable reporting standard(s). Responsibilities will include gathering relevant production and emissions data (or other environmental data) from applicable internal operations/procurement teams and compilation of footprint information consistent with customer requirements. Work may include filing customer-specific forms and establishing long term footprint reduction forecasts.
Continuously improve accuracy, efficiency, transparency, and auditability of sustainability database. Work will include assisting with development of long-term strategy to evolve database from existing disparate excel-based data environment to a concisely structured common excel-based environment, then eventually to a sustainability software environment. Other aspects will include establishing clear emissions/conversion factor repository basis documentation, improved calculation formulations, audit trail documentation, improved integration with other existing Materion ERPs. Also review of historical database accuracy and resolution of errors or outliers. Assist with screening of future software platforms, conversion of data into new platform, and then administration of new platform.
Miscellaneous sustainability program support including development of charts, analytics or other data for presentations, participation in calls, coordination with consultants, contributing to development of sustainability reports or webpage updates, completion of external customer data surveys, development of internal data surveys, participation in audits, assist with development of climate or other goals, tracking progress versus goals, etc. External (Scope 3) supply chain environmental data gathering will become a growing priority and time requirement in future years.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Science, Engineering Technology, Sustainability, Data Analytics, Financial Analytics, or a related field is preferred. An Associate’s degree may be considered with demonstrated equivalent industry experience in sustainability or data analytics.
Sustainability or Environmental-emphasis educational experience.
2-5 years of experience in complex data analysis and reporting required.
2-5 years of sustainability data analysis and reporting under industry/regulatory frameworks (GRI, SASB, TCFD, CBAM, GHG Protocol, LCA, PCF, etc) preferred.
Advanced proficiency with large sets Microsoft Excel data (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP, XLOOKUP, INDIRECT, INDEX/MATCH, Power Query, formulas, scripts, charts).
Strong analytical, quantitative, and problem-solving skills with a keen attention to detail.
Ability to learn and apply basic engineering calculations, including energy and mass balances, unit conversions, and use of emissions factors.
Ability to collaborate effectively with diverse range of stakeholders, including internal operational teams.
Prior experience with sustainability data analytics and reporting preferred.
Experience with business intelligence tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau), sustainability data management software, macros/VBA and data management software preferred.

Job Summary:

Collects, analyzes, and interprets operational data to improve distribution performance and support data-driven decision-making. Focuses on executing well-defined analytic work with growing autonomy. This involves creating new analyses, and dashboards, or updating more complex dashboards/reporting. The role develops reports, KPIs, dashboards, and analytical tools using SQL and data visualization platforms such as Tableau or Power BI. This position collaborates closely with management and business partners to define analytical requirements, recommend solutions, and communicate insights to both technical and non-technical audiences. Strong analytical reasoning, data storytelling, and attention to data quality are essential. This role provides mentorship to junior analysts.

Responsibilities

Collect, cleanse, transform, and analyze large operational datasets to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for improvement.
Develop, enhance, and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations using Tableau Desktop or similar tools.
Apply statistical and analytical techniques to perform descriptive, diagnostic, and trend-based analysis.
Translate business questions into structured analytical approaches and clearly communicate findings and recommendations.
Validate and quality-check data sources, queries, and visualizations to ensure accuracy, consistency, and reliability.
Partner with cross-functional teams to understand operational needs and deliver analytical solutions aligned with business priorities.
Support the implementation of improved reporting methodologies, automation, or analytical tools.
Maintain documentation and adhere to established departmental, IT, and project management standards.
Prioritize and manage multiple analytical requests in alignment with business objectives and timelines.
The role progresses through impact, autonomy, and analytical complexity, as well as ability to handle sensitive information.

Travel:

Occasional travel may be required (5–10%+ annually). This is an on-site position requiring daily in-office attendance Monday through Friday, and as needed.

Qualifications

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:

Required:

Bachelor’s degree in a related field required (Data Analytics, Data Science, Computer Science, Math, Cyber Security, Information Technology, etc.), or 5+ years of relevant experience.
Relevant experience with Tableau or PowerBI required.
Preferred experience in a warehouse setting or manufacturing.
1-3+ years of experience in SQL, including queries, joins, transformations, and data aggregation.
1-3+ years of experience with Tableau Desktop, PowerBI, or equivalent data visualization tools.
Entry-level to moderate experience performing data analysis and reporting, and utilizing analytical and problem-solving skills.
Working knowledge of data warehousing concepts, data modeling, and ETL processes.
Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools, including Excel and SharePoint.
Ability to follow standardized processes related to project management, data governance, and IT security.
Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences.

Preferred:

Experience with Snowflake, Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS), Oracle GoldenGate, DBeaver, etc.
Experience with Tableau Prep, Alteryx, or similar data preparation tools.
Experience using Python or R for analytics or automation.
Experience working within a team, and with other teams or departments.

Work Environment:

Normal warehouse and office environment with some exposure to dust, heat, and cold.
May include prolonged sitting in front of a computer.

We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender, gender identity or expression, or veteran status. We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace.

Job Title:
Business Data Analyst

Location:
Upper Marlboro, MD (On-site)

Department:
Finance Operations & Analytics

Reports To:
Finance Operations & Analytics Manager

Job Summary

We are seeking a Business Data Analyst to join our Finance Operations & Analytics team in Upper Marlboro, MD. This role will focus on reporting automation, data visualization, and AI-driven insights to support data-informed decision-making in our finance and operations functions. The Business Analyst will develop and automate financial and operational reports, build interactive dashboards in Power BI and advanced Excel models, and leverage AI tools to uncover actionable insights. The Business Analyst will support broader finance operations initiatives by providing analytical expertise and process improvements across the organization. The ideal candidate is a collaborative, detail-oriented professional with strong analytical capabilities, excellent communication skills, and a drive to continuously improve business processes through data and technology.

Key Responsibilities

Reporting Automation: Identify and implement opportunities to automate recurring financial and operational reporting processes, reducing manual work and improving efficiency. This includes developing scheduled automated workflows (e.g., using Power Query or scripting) for regular reports and ensuring data refreshes are timely and accurate.
Data Visualization & Dashboard Development: Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and data visualizations using Microsoft Power BI and Excel to track key performance indicators (KPIs), financial metrics, and operational trends. Ensure that reports and visuals effectively communicate insights and support management in understanding business performance.
AI-Driven Insights & Advanced Analytics: Leverage AI and advanced analytics tools (such as machine learning models or predictive analytics software) to analyze large datasets and generate forward-looking insights (e.g., trend analysis, forecasts, anomaly detection) that inform strategic decisions in finance and operations. Stay current with emerging AI technologies and evaluate their potential to enhance reporting processes and business intelligence capabilities.
Data Analysis & Insight Generation: Gather, analyze, and interpret financial and operational data from various sources to identify patterns, correlations, and opportunities for improvement. Provide actionable insights through data analysis and clearly communicate findings to stakeholders, translating complex data into business recommendations.
Collaboration & Stakeholder Engagement: Work closely with cross-functional teams – including finance, operations, IT, and key stakeholders – to understand reporting needs, define business requirements, and ensure solutions meet those needs. This includes partnering with other Data Analysts on data collection, validation, and analysis efforts to support broad finance operations initiatives and organizational goals.
Process Improvement: Analyze current finance operations processes (such as budgeting, forecasting, expense tracking, and financial reporting) to identify process inefficiencies or bottlenecks. Recommend and help implement improvements or automation (potentially utilizing AI tools or macros) to enhance the accuracy, speed, and efficiency of financial operations and reporting.
Report Documentation & Supporting Materials: Develop and maintain clear documentation for all reporting processes, data definitions, and dashboard usage guidelines to ensure knowledge transfer and continuity. Train team members and stakeholders on using new reports or analytical tools as needed.
Compliance & Data Integrity: Ensure data accuracy, quality, and security in all reporting activities. Support data governance efforts by validating data against source systems and working with IT or data engineers to resolve data quality issues. Assist in internal or external audits by providing required data and documentation, ensuring that reports comply with company policies and financial controls.
Continuous Learning: Continually monitor industry trends and best practices in financial analytics, business intelligence, and AI applications in finance. Proactively propose innovative solutions or tools (e.g., improved visualization techniques, new analytics platforms) that could benefit the Finance Operations & Analytics team and the organization’s decision-making capabilities.

Required Qualifications

Education & Experience: Bachelor’s degree or 4 years of Data Analytics experience.
Technical Skills: Proficiency in Microsoft Power BI and Excel is required, with a proven ability to create complex spreadsheets, pivot tables, macros, and interactive dashboards. Familiarity with data analysis techniques (e.g., data modeling, trend analysis) and experience in building reports and visualizations for business or finance operations.
Analytical Abilities: Strong quantitative and analytical skills, with experience interpreting large datasets to derive insights and support decision-making. Ability to perform data validation, detect anomalies or trends, and translate data into meaningful conclusions for business strategies.
Communication Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to present findings and complex analysis in a clear, concise manner to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Collaboration: Demonstrated ability to work effectively in a cross-functional team environment, collaborating with colleagues from finance, operations, IT, and other departments. A track record of building positive working relationships and influencing others to achieve project goals.
Attention to Detail: Strong commitment to accuracy and detail in data handling, report generation, and documentation. Must be able to ensure data quality and consistency across all reports.
Problem-Solving: Proven ability to approach challenges with a problem-solving mindset. Capable of identifying the root cause of business issues and recommending data-driven solutions or process improvements.

Summary: $75,000 - $90,000 with bonus potential
